_README: Critter species. Each has a `role` (fighter/enhancer/chef/gatherer) and a `proficiency` value (1-100). Proficiency drives everything: production rate, patrol HP, craft speed. Grows +1 per level. Base scales with rarity: Common 3-6, Uncommon 7-10, Rare 11-16, Legendary 17-24. Boss critters have 2x proficiency.

mossbun.name: Mossbun
mossbun.desc: A gentle grass critter. Great forager.
mossbun.lore: Mossbuns are the first creatures most colonists befriend. Their soft moss-covered fur absorbs morning dew, which they share with nearby crops. Colonies with Mossbuns report harvests twice as lush. They hum a low melody at dawn that other critters find deeply calming.

pebblit.name: Pebblit
pebblit.desc: Tough little rock critter. Born to mine.
pebblit.lore: Pebblits are living stones that roll downhill when excited. They instinctively headbutt boulders, which is annoying until you realize they're actually mining for you. A Pebblit colony can tunnel through solid granite in days. Their rocky shells get shinier with age.

flickwing.name: Flickwing
flickwing.desc: Fast and nimble. Excellent at crafting.
flickwing.lore: Flickwings are tiny moth-like critters whose wings shimmer with arcane dust. They're obsessed with shiny objects and will spend hours organizing gems by color. Master crafters prize their precision.

thornback.name: Thornback
thornback.desc: Spiky hedgehog critter. Defends the colony.
thornback.lore: Thornbacks curl into spiked balls when threatened, making them nearly impossible to eat. They forage for roots and berries, storing excess in hollow stumps they mark with thorns. Fighters love them — they eat the pests that plague crops.

mudgrub.name: Mudgrub
mudgrub.desc: Ugly worm thing. Tanky and persistent.
mudgrub.lore: Nobody knows what Mudgrubs actually are. They're not worms, not slugs, and definitely not pleasant to look at. But they refuse to die. Drop one off a cliff and it bounces. Hit it with a rock and it absorbs the rock. Perfect miners.

dustmite.name: Dustmite
dustmite.desc: Tiny dust bug. Lucky charms for nearby crafters.
dustmite.lore: Dustmites vibrate at frequencies that make them blur. Catching one is like grabbing smoke. They seem to attract good fortune — colonists report finding rare gems and extra resources whenever Dustmites are nearby.

puffshroom.name: Puffshroom
puffshroom.lore: Puffshrooms are sentient fungi that waddle around on stubby root-legs. They regenerate from nearly any wound by absorbing nutrients from the soil. When happy, they release a cloud of pink spores that smells like fresh bread.

scraprat.name: Scraprat
scraprat.lore: Scraprats build elaborate nests from discarded materials — bottle caps, bent nails, old gears. What looks like junk to us is architecture to them. They're surprisingly useful at mines and workbenches. Just don't leave food unattended.

glowmite.name: Glowmite
glowmite.desc: A mysterious luminous critter. Amplifies magic.
glowmite.lore: Glowmites hover in clusters that form constellation-like patterns at night. Ancient texts describe them as 'living stars fallen from the research of forgotten gods.' Their bioluminescence resonates with arcane crystals, amplifying the Doctrine Building's output.

emberfox.name: Emberfox
emberfox.lore: Emberfoxes leave trails of harmless sparks when they run. Their body temperature runs hot enough to smelt low-grade ore just by sitting on it. In the wild, they hunt in pairs — one flushes prey while the other ambushes. Tamed Emberfoxes are fiercely loyal.

crystalhorn.name: Crystalhorn
crystalhorn.lore: Crystalhorns grow mineral deposits on their shells throughout their lives. The oldest specimens have shells worth a fortune. They eat stone and excrete purified ore — nature's perfect mining machine. Their horn vibrates at a frequency that shatters rock.

bogwalker.name: Bogwalker
bogwalker.desc: Massive swamp toad. Hits like a truck. Swims.
bogwalker.lore: Bogwalkers are patient hunters that can sit motionless for days. When prey wanders close, they strike with a tongue that can shatter bone. Despite their terrifying power, tamed Bogwalkers are gentle giants.

sparkfly.name: Sparkfly
sparkfly.desc: Tiny electric firefly. Boosts nearby critters.
sparkfly.lore: Sparkflies generate bioelectric fields that make them untouchable in the wild. They communicate through synchronized light patterns — a swarm of Sparkflies looks like a dancing aurora. They can power small machines with their static charge.

rotjaw.name: Rotjaw
rotjaw.lore: Rotjaws are survivors from a bygone era — their ancestors were apex predators. Modern Rotjaws are smaller but no less vicious. Their bite carries a mild venom that makes wounds itch for weeks.

stormwing.name: Stormwing
stormwing.lore: Stormwings ride atmospheric currents at impossible speeds. During thunderstorms, they absorb lightning and store it in their crystalline feathers. A single Stormwing's discharge can power a colony for hours.

ironshell.name: Ironshell
ironshell.lore: Ironshells are living fortresses. Their shells are composed of naturally occurring steel alloys that only get harder with age. An Ironshell on patrol can absorb hits that would flatten buildings.

venomaw.name: Venomaw
venomaw.desc: Toxic frog. Strangely excellent at cooking.
venomaw.lore: Venomaws secrete a psychoactive toxin that causes vivid hallucinations. Strangely, dishes they prepare taste incredible — the toxin neutralizes in heat, leaving only complex flavors. Master chefs swear by them.

goretusk.name: Goretusk
goretusk.lore: Goretusks are walking natural disasters. They charge at anything that moves and destroy anything that doesn't. Their tusks can pierce reinforced walls. As bodyguards, they're unmatched.

faewisp.name: Faewisp
faewisp.lore: Faewisps are fragments of pure magical energy that somehow achieved sentience. They flicker between visible and invisible, communicating through emotions rather than sound. Their presence accelerates all arcane work to miraculous speeds.

shadowfang.name: Shadowfang
shadowfang.desc: Dark wolf of shadow. Apex combat beast.
shadowfang.lore: Shadowfangs are apex predators from the void between biomes. They phase through solid matter and strike from impossible angles. A bonded Shadowfang has never been known to let its master die.

celestine.name: Celestine
celestine.lore: Celestines are believed to be physical manifestations of the world's healing magic. Where they walk, flowers bloom. Where they rest, wounds close. A single Celestine in a Healing Hut is worth ten mundane healers.

dreadmaw.name: Dreadmaw
dreadmaw.desc: Abyssal horror. Eats anything — including ore.
dreadmaw.lore: No one knows where Dreadmaws come from. They appear from underground, consume everything in their path, then vanish. Their stomach acid dissolves diamond. Unmatched at deep-earth extraction.

pixibell.name: Pixibell
pixibell.lore: Pixibells are impossibly rare golden sprites that leave trails of golden dust. Everything they touch turns profitable — ore yields double, crafts produce extras, and rare materials appear from nowhere.
